items.title: It speaks volumes about Dillon Mitchell that upon transferring to St. John's he was named a team captain.
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
It speaks volumes about Dillon Mitchell that upon transferring to St. John's he was named a team captain.

It was the third destination the former McDonald's All-American had to start from scratch and prove himself at.

What did that teach the Celtics' rookie about himself?

items.title: Get to know Celtics' rookie Dillon Mitchell.
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
Get to know Celtics' rookie Dillon Mitchell.

From what he learned about himself along his basketball voyage to what he might be doing if he wasn't in the NBA, favorite memories under Rick Pitino, & something he can't wait to do when he gets to Boston.

Mitchell also has a message for Celtics fans:

items.title: EXCLUSIVE: Dillon Mitchell
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
EXCLUSIVE: Dillon Mitchell

Get to know the Celtics' second-round pick on and off the court.

The former St. John's captain is a high-character, passionate individual built to thrive in the Celtics' elite player development program.

"Willing to die for it." hardwoodhoudini.com/inside-minds...

items.title: Celtics second round pick Dillon Mitchell tonight: 24/8/2 and 8 stocks including more made 3s than in his senior year of college.

items.title: After arriving in Boston on Monday, Chris Cenac Jr. and Dillon Mitchell hopped off the plane and went straight to the gym.
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
After arriving in Boston on Monday, Chris Cenac Jr. and Dillon Mitchell hopped off the plane and went straight to the gym.

Tuesday, they took part in an annual tradition for the Celtics' draft class, starting their careers with an early opportunity to connect with the community.

Afterward...1/2

items.title: Chris Cenac Jr. & Dillon Mitchell took part in an annual tradition for the Celtics' draft class. Their careers in Boston start by connecting with the community.
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
Chris Cenac Jr. & Dillon Mitchell took part in an annual tradition for the Celtics' draft class. Their careers in Boston start by connecting with the community.

"It feels like home already."

Cenac also shared why Kelvin Sampson's hard-nosed coaching brought the best out of him, delivering a...1/2

items.title: Jayson Tatum was the first one to reach out to Dillon Mitchell after the Celtics drafted him.
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
Jayson Tatum was the first one to reach out to Dillon Mitchell after the Celtics drafted him.

Mitchell also heard from Derrick White and Jordan Walsh, among others. Him and Walsh were in the same high school class, so they’ve known each other for a bit.

items.title: How would Celtics’ rookie Dillon Mitchell describe his game?
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
How would Celtics’ rookie Dillon Mitchell describe his game?

“Defense, toughness, do whatever it takes to win. Be a glue guy and just compete. The only thing around here is winning.”

items.title: I also asked Dillon Mitchell about how his time at St. John’s helped him grow as a passer.
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
I also asked Dillon Mitchell about how his time at St. John’s helped him grow as a passer.

“I think it came down to that we didn't really have like a true point guard last year. So, in practice, he kind of seen me rebound the ball, push it, try to make plays.

“I'm a very selfless person. I…” 1/x

items.title: Dillon Mitchell was one of the best perimeter defenders in college basketball.
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
Dillon Mitchell was one of the best perimeter defenders in college basketball.

I asked Mitchell who he studies on that end of the floor. He pointed to Jaden McDaniels and Jared Vanderbilt.

Mitchell also cited the importance of understanding player tendencies.

items.title: Dillon Mitchell on getting drafted by the Celtics:
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
Dillon Mitchell on getting drafted by the Celtics:

“When you get that phone call, lot of emotions, lot of excitement, just being here, being a part of it, knowing that they believe in me so much.

“I felt that since, honestly, when I came to work during pre-draft.”

items.title: Chris Cenac Jr., who, along with Dillon Mitchell, arrived in Boston yesterday, on getting introduced to those within the Celtics’ organization:
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
Chris Cenac Jr., who, along with Dillon Mitchell, arrived in Boston yesterday, on getting introduced to those within the Celtics’ organization:

“It feels like home already.”

items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
Mike Zarren remembers first watching Dillon Mitchell play at Texas. He was raw, but his athleticism stood out.

Under Rick Pitino, he found a role he could excel in, starring on defense and showing exciting growth as a passer.

1/2

items.title: Only the Hornets and Nuggets averaged fewer steals per game last season than the Boston Celtics. Dillon Mitchell had 47 steals in 37 games at St. John's last season.

items.title: Dillon Mitchell is an outstanding defender. He's a very good rebounder for his size. He can pass the ball.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Dillon Mitchell is an outstanding defender. He's a very good rebounder for his size. He can pass the ball.

The challenge is that Mitchell can't shoot. Not even a little bit. Curious to see how he fits as a wing in Boston's system.

items.title: The Celtics select Dillon Mitchell with pick No. 40.
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
The Celtics select Dillon Mitchell with pick No. 40.

Mitchell is an elite athlete and defender. He's a skilled passer as well. Hasn't shown much as a shooter or scorer.
